brief_title: Incidence and Patterns of Nausea/Vomiting With Combined Chemotherapy and Radiation
official_title: A Prospective Study to Evaluate the Incidence and Patterns of Nausea and Vomiting in Patients Receiving Combined Chemotherapy and Radiation
brief_summary: Questionnaire study to observe the incidence and pattern or nausea and vomiting in patients receiving combined chemotherapy and radiation. 83% of patients experience radiation therapy-induced vomiting; significant nausea and vomiting could develop with concurrent chemotherapy despite standard anti-nauseous medication prophylaxis.
detailed_description: Questionnaires will be answered daily on a weekly basis during period of radiation and chemotherapy.
conditions: Cancer of the Head and Neck

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Concurrent radiation and chemotherapy treatment
* Aerodigestive malignancy

Exclusion Criteria:

* Other causes of vomiting (i.e.gastrointestinal obstruction...)

primary_outcomes.measure: Assess incidence and patterns of nausea and vomiting in patients undergoing concurrent chemotherapy and radiation
